case_title: SHANNON E. LUBAR VS. SAM AWRABI

docket_entries.description: UNDER APPLICATION TO THE CLERK FOR JUDGMENT UNDER SEC 1710.25 C.C.P., IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF LUBAR, SHANNON E. RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT AWRABI, SAM $2,733.00 PRINCIPAL, $225.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $2,958.00, SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT ALL FURTHER PROCESSING TO BE DONE BY DEFAULT SECTION
EXECUTION FOR MONEY ISSUED TO SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY AS TO DEFENDANT AWRABI, SAM

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT, MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES AND DECLARATION OF DEFENDANT (TRANSACTION ID # 17348024) FILED BY DEFENDANT AWRABI, SAM HEARING SET FOR JAN-19-2018 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAM AWRABI'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS DENIED. SUBSTITUTE SERVICE WAS COMPLETE ON OCTOBER 10, 2017 PER CCP 415.20(B). DEFENDANT HAD THROUGH NOVEMBER 8, 2017 TO FILE A MOTION TO VACATE THE JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 1710.40(B). DEFENDANT FILED THE INSTANT APPLICATION IN AN UNTIMELY FASHION ON DECEMBER 15, 2017. THEREFORE, THE MOTION IS DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MITCH LYON, CSR#6840, MLYON@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. (D302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Defendant
Off Calendar Jan-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sam Awrabi 559-892-5699 Defendant in Pro Per. (appears late at 9:37 am after the Court has ruled and signed order.)

The matter is recalled.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant informs it has notified the other side but did not send any email to contest the tentative ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAM AWRABI's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment is DENIED. Substitute service was complete on October 10, 2017 per CCP 415.20(b). Defendant had through November 8, 2017 to file a motion to vacate the judgment pursuant to CCP 1710.40(b). Defendant filed the instant application in an untimely fashion on December 15, 2017. Therefore, the motion is denied.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840, mlyon@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RBU)
